findings.title: WorkspaceContext contract is too underspecified to design safely
findings.detail: The proposal says WorkspaceContext will resolve repo root, branch name, repo identity, worktree path, and diff, but it does not define the behavioral contract designers need to target: which values are required vs optional, what happens in detached HEAD or non-git workspaces, how failures are surfaced, and who constructs/injects the implementation in local vs external runtimes. Add explicit contract-level requirements and failure/fallback behavior so multiple implementations can remain compatible.

findings.title: Diff abstraction does not capture the semantics that must be preserved
findings.detail: Moving specflow-filter-diff and review-orchestration onto WorkspaceContext is directionally correct, but the proposal never states what diff is being abstracted. Design cannot proceed safely without defining the source and scope of the diff (for example working tree vs staged vs merge-base, path filtering, target refs, empty diff behavior, and compatibility expectations for existing review flows). Document the required diff inputs/outputs and invariants that the new interface must preserve.

findings.title: Acceptance criteria are not specific enough to validate parity or extensibility
findings.detail: The only explicit success condition is that there is no CLI breaking change, which is too broad to validate against during design and implementation. Add concrete acceptance criteria for local parity and extension behavior, such as run-start metadata fields remaining equivalent, review-orchestration receiving the same diff semantics as today, core no longer importing git-specific logic directly, and alternate WorkspaceContext implementations being injectable without local-mode assumptions.

findings.title: WorkspaceContext scope is inconsistent with its stated role as an external-runtime abstraction
findings.detail: The proposal positions WorkspaceContext as the extension point that lets external runtimes provide alternate implementations, but the interface contract itself is still tightly coupled to local git semantics: filteredDiff takes git pathspec exclusions, diff behavior is defined in terms of raw git commands, repoIdentity is fixed to owner/repo, and the contract says non-git workspaces fail at construction. That leaves a core design ambiguity: is this boundary intentionally git-only, or is it meant to be runtime-neutral? Resolve that before handoff by either explicitly narrowing scope to a git-only abstraction and deferring generic external-runtime support, or by redefining the interface in runtime-neutral terms and moving git-specific semantics into LocalWorkspaceContext only.

findings.title: WorkspaceContext no longer exposes a stable source for existing repo_name metadata
findings.detail: Acceptance Criterion 1 requires preserving the existing `repo_name` value, but the interface only exposes `projectIdentity()` with an implementation-defined format (`owner/repo`, URL, UUID, etc.). That forces core code either to parse backend-specific identity strings or to change the stored metadata shape, neither of which is specified. The local implementation also derives identity only from `origin`, so behavior for git repos without that remote is undefined despite the 'No CLI breaking change' requirement. Define how `repo_name` is produced in a runtime-neutral way, or add a separate name/display field plus an explicit local fallback or support constraint.

findings.title: filteredDiff semantics are still undefined at the contract level
findings.detail: The revised interface defines the `filteredDiff()` return shape, but it never states what change set the diff represents. The local section mentions `git diff` commands, yet the contract does not say whether the baseline is HEAD, index, or something else, nor whether staged, unstaged, renamed, and untracked changes must be included. That leaves alternate WorkspaceContext implementations unable to produce compatible behavior and makes Acceptance Criterion 2 impossible to validate consistently. Specify the runtime-neutral meaning of the diff and its inclusion rules in the interface contract, with git commands described only as one implementation strategy.

findings.title: Local filteredDiff algorithm omits staged-only changes
findings.detail: The contract now says filteredDiff() must return all uncommitted changes since the last committed snapshot, but the proposed local algorithm uses `git diff --name-status -M100` and `git diff --`, which compare the working tree against the index and therefore miss staged-only modifications, additions, and deletions. That contradiction makes Acceptance Criterion 2 unattainable as written. Specify a HEAD-based diff strategy, or an explicit combination of cached and uncached diffs, so the local implementation matches the declared contract.

findings.title: Local projectIdentity derivation does not satisfy the stated uniqueness contract
findings.detail: The interface defines `projectIdentity()` as a required unique, stable project identifier, but `LocalWorkspaceContext` derives it from `owner/repo` or `local/<directory-name>`. Those values can collide across different hosts or unrelated local repos, and the directory-name fallback changes if the repo is renamed or moved. Define the intended uniqueness scope and specify an identity derivation that actually satisfies it, or explicitly constrain/error on cases where no stable identifier is available.
findings.notes: Override: MEDIUM. projectIdentity の一意性スコープと具体的なフォールバック戦略は design フェーズで決定する。proposal レベルでは interface contract の定義が主要スコープであり、実装のフォールバック詳細は design で解決可能。
